name: Alaska Seafood Tarts
recipeInstructions:
1. Pre-heat oven to 80°C, 350°F, Gas mark 4.
2. Lightly grease 8 individual pie dishes or ovenproof pudding bowls.
3. Drain the can of salmon and mix the juice with water until it reaches a total volume of about 200ml/7fl.oz for fish stock. Flake the salmon and set aside.
4. Brush each individual sheet of filo pastry with oil and fold into sixteen 12.5cm/5-inch squares. Place one square in each pie dish, leaving the pointed corners protruding over the edge.
5. Brush with oil, then place a second square of pastry on top, but with the corners pointing up between the original ones to create a water lily effect. Brush the points well with oil.
6. Bake for 5 minutes to set but not brown. Remove from the oven.
7. Reduce the oven temperature to 150°C, 300°F, Gas mark 2. Melt the margarine and stir in the flour. Blend in the fish stock, beating well to remove lumps. Stir in the yogurt, seafood sticks, walnuts, and flaked salmon into the sauce. Divide equally between the 8 pastry cases.
8. Sprinkle the breadcrumbs over the top, then return to the oven to heat through for 5-8 minutes or until the cheese and pastry have turned golden brown. Serve immediately.
